name: Irish Soda Bread#1
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly grease a heavy skillet for soda farl or preheat the oven to 450°F for soda cake.
2. Sift the dry ingredients into a bowl, ensuring even distribution of bicarbonate of soda.
3. Make a well in the center and add about half the buttermilk.
4. Stir until you have a raggy dough that is very squishy but looks mostly dry.
5. Add more liquid sparingly until you achieve the desired texture.
6. Blend all ingredients together; turn out onto a lightly floured board.
7. Knead for no more than 1 minute or 1 minute and a half. Overkneading makes the bread tough.
8. If making soda cake, shape into a circle about 8-9 inches across and 1 inch thick. Cut a cross about halfway deep on top.
9. Place on a floured baking sheet and bake at 450°F for 45-50 minutes.
10. If making soda farl, shape into a circle about 9 inches by 1 inch thick, cut into four wedges, and cook slowly over low-to-medium heat until puffed and lightly browned, about 20 minutes total.
